category_title: Elementary students with anger management skill deficits and bullying behaviors
minimal_context: Amy is working on a poster with two peers using art supplies during school hours.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Amy, please work with the two other students at your table to share the art supplies and finish the poster.

STUDENT: They aren't doing it the way I want. I am getting angry because I feel out of control of the project.

TUTOR: You just need to figure it out and keep working together.

STUDENT: I don't want to do this anymore. I am always the one 'getting in trouble' when things go wrong.

TUTOR: Please just focus on the assignment and talk to your group.

STUDENT: Shut up! If you two touch the art supplies again, I am going to beat you both up after school!

options.A: Place special emphasis on the developmental integration of affect, the vocabulary of emotion, and cognitive understanding during school hours.
options.B: Implement group activities and poster projects that feature less demanding rules for peer interaction during school hours.
options.C: Leverage the student's interest in art to create a 'Ways to Calm Down' poster to present during a class lesson.
options.D: Provide individual learning opportunities for the student to complete her assignments and poster projects during school hours.
